criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking trade allocations as a distinct allegation category with its own dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations.' The output describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP (68.4% profitable rate) and Chao's personal account (81.6% profitable rate) while directing unprofitable trades to other clients, compared to the 41.2% rate for other client accounts. The allegation is further elaborated in the 'Cherry-Picking Pattern Harm to Clients' section, which describes the systematic allocation scheme and its financial impact.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly cites Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the output states: 'The Division alleges a pattern of cherry-picking in violation of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act.'

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the output states that 'WCA, through the actions and omissions of Whitmore and Chao, systematically allocated same-day profitable trades to favored accounts' and references WCA's failure to maintain contemporaneous allocation records. The 'Cherry-Picking Pattern Harm to Clients' section also explicitly states 'WCA, through the actions and omissions of Whitmore and Chao, systematically allocated same-day profitable trades to favored accounts.' WCA is identified as the firm responsible for the cherry-picking scheme throughout the output.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output identifies Whitmore as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation. The output thus identifies Whitmore as implicated in cherry-picking due to his 12% LP interest in Oceanic Fund I LP.
